datasets.name: Jigsaw Toxic Comments
datasets.note: 160K Wikipedia talk page comments classified across 6 toxicity categories. The founding multi-label content moderation dataset.

categories.name: toxic
categories.definition: Comments that are rude, disrespectful, or otherwise likely to make someone leave a discussion.

categories.name: severe toxic
categories.definition: Comments that are very hateful, aggressive, or disrespectful to an extreme degree.

categories.name: obscene
categories.definition: Comments containing obscene or vulgar language.

categories.name: threat
categories.definition: Comments containing threats of violence or harm.

categories.name: insult
categories.definition: Comments intended to insult or demean someone.

categories.name: identity hate
categories.definition: Comments that express hatred toward a person based on identity (race, religion, gender, etc.).

categories.name: sexual content
categories.definition: Content meant to arouse sexual excitement, such as the description of sexual activity, or that promotes sexual services (excluding sex education and wellness).

categories.name: hate speech
categories.definition: Content that expresses, incites, or promotes hate based on race, gender, ethnicity, religion, nationality, sexual orientation, disability status, or caste.

categories.name: violence
categories.definition: Content that promotes or glorifies violence or celebrates the suffering or humiliation of others.

categories.name: harassment
categories.definition: Content that may be used to torment or annoy individuals in real life, or make harassment more likely to occur.

categories.name: self-harm
categories.definition: Content that promotes, encourages, or depicts acts of self-harm, such as suicide, cutting, and eating disorders.

categories.name: sexual/minors
categories.definition: Sexual content that includes an individual who is under 18 years old.

categories.name: hate/threatening
categories.definition: Hateful content that also includes violence or serious harm towards the targeted group.

categories.name: violence/graphic
categories.definition: Violent content that depicts death, violence, or serious physical injury in extreme graphic detail.

datasets.name: BeaverTails
datasets.note: 330K prompt-response pairs classified across 14 harm categories by PKU.

categories.name: animal abuse
categories.definition: Content that promotes or depicts cruelty, harm, or neglect toward animals.

categories.name: child abuse
categories.definition: Content involving harm, exploitation, or endangerment of minors.

categories.name: controversial topics/politics
categories.definition: Content involving divisive political, social, or ideological topics.

categories.name: discrimination/stereotype
categories.definition: Content that promotes discrimination, stereotypes, or injustice based on group identity.

categories.name: drug abuse/weapons
categories.definition: Content promoting drug abuse, weapons, or banned substances.

categories.name: financial crime/theft
categories.definition: Content related to financial crimes, property crimes, or theft.

categories.name: hate speech/offensive
categories.definition: Content containing hate speech or offensive language targeting groups or individuals.

categories.name: misinformation
categories.definition: Misinformation regarding ethics, laws, and safety topics.

categories.name: non-violent unethical behavior
categories.definition: Content promoting unethical behavior that does not involve physical violence.

categories.name: privacy violation
categories.definition: Content that violates or encourages violation of personal privacy.

categories.name: self-harm
categories.definition: Content that promotes, encourages, or depicts acts of self-harm.

categories.name: sexually explicit/adult
categories.definition: Sexually explicit or adult content.

categories.name: terrorism/organized crime
categories.definition: Content related to terrorism or organized criminal activities.

categories.name: violence/incitement
categories.definition: Content that promotes violence, aiding and abetting, or incitement to violence.

datasets.name: PKU-SafeRLHF
datasets.note: 265K preference pairs with 19 harm categories and 3-level severity. Extends BeaverTails with cybercrime, mental manipulation, and environmental damage.

categories.name: animal abuse
categories.definition: Content that promotes or depicts cruelty, harm, or neglect toward animals.

categories.name: cybercrime
categories.definition: Content related to hacking, phishing, malware, or other computer-based crimes.

categories.name: copyright issues
categories.definition: Content involving copyright infringement or intellectual property violations.

categories.name: discriminatory behavior
categories.definition: Content promoting discrimination based on identity characteristics.

categories.name: drugs
categories.definition: Content promoting drug use or production of controlled substances.

categories.name: economic crime
categories.definition: Content related to fraud, embezzlement, or financial crimes.

categories.name: environmental damage
categories.definition: Content promoting or facilitating environmental harm or pollution.

categories.name: human trafficking
categories.definition: Content related to human trafficking, forced labor, or modern slavery.

categories.name: insulting behavior
categories.definition: Content intended to insult, demean, or belittle individuals.

categories.name: mental manipulation
categories.definition: Content designed to psychologically manipulate, gaslight, or coerce people.

categories.name: endangering national security
categories.definition: Content that threatens national security or promotes espionage.

categories.name: endangering public health
categories.definition: Content that endangers public health through misinformation or harmful advice.

categories.name: disrupting public order
categories.definition: Content that promotes disruption of public order or civil unrest.

categories.name: psychological harm
categories.definition: Content that causes or promotes psychological distress, trauma, or emotional harm.

categories.name: privacy violation
categories.definition: Content that violates or encourages violation of personal privacy.

categories.name: physical harm
categories.definition: Content that promotes or facilitates physical harm to individuals.

categories.name: sexual content
categories.definition: Sexually explicit or suggestive content.

categories.name: violence
categories.definition: Content depicting or promoting violence.

categories.name: white-collar crime
categories.definition: Content related to corporate fraud, insider trading, or business-related crimes.

datasets.name: NVIDIA Aegis v2
datasets.note: 29K human-annotated prompt-response pairs across 23 safety categories.

categories.name: copyright/trademark/plagiarism
categories.definition: Content involving copyright infringement, trademark violations, or plagiarism.

categories.name: criminal planning/confessions
categories.definition: Content involving planning or confessing to criminal activities.

categories.name: controlled/regulated substances
categories.definition: Content related to controlled or regulated substances.

categories.name: fraud/deception
categories.definition: Content involving fraudulent schemes or deceptive practices.

categories.name: high-risk gov. decisions
categories.definition: Content related to high-risk government decision-making processes.

categories.name: guns/illegal weapons
categories.definition: Content related to guns and illegal weapons.

categories.name: harassment
categories.definition: Content intended to harass, bully, or intimidate individuals.

categories.name: hate/identity hate
categories.definition: Content expressing hate based on identity characteristics.

categories.name: illegal activity
categories.definition: Content promoting or describing general illegal activities.

categories.name: immoral/unethical
categories.definition: Content promoting immoral or unethical behavior.

categories.name: manipulation
categories.definition: Content designed to manipulate or deceive people.

categories.name: malware
categories.definition: Content related to creating or distributing malicious software.

categories.name: needs caution
categories.definition: Content that requires careful handling but may not be explicitly harmful.

categories.name: other
categories.definition: Content flagged for safety concerns not covered by other categories.

categories.name: profanity
categories.definition: Content containing profane or vulgar language.

categories.name: PII/privacy
categories.definition: Content involving personally identifiable information or privacy violations.

categories.name: political/misinfo/conspiracy
categories.definition: Content involving political misinformation or conspiracy theories.

categories.name: sexual (minor)
categories.definition: Sexual content involving minors.

categories.name: suicide/self-harm
categories.definition: Content related to suicide or self-harm.

categories.name: sexual
categories.definition: Sexually explicit or suggestive content.

categories.name: threat
categories.definition: Content containing threats of violence or harm.

categories.name: unauthorized advice
categories.definition: Content providing unauthorized professional advice (legal, medical, financial).

categories.name: violence
categories.definition: Content depicting or promoting violence.
